steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between two complete thoughts: "Okinaka doesn't make such decisions [blank]" and "all historical designations must be approved by a group of nine other experts." Both sides are independent clauses, so the blank must supply punctuation that legally joins them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'single-handedly and': "and" is a coordinating conjunction, but joining two independent clauses with a coordinating conjunction requires a comma before it. There is no comma here, so this is a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — 'single-handedly,': A comma alone between two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'single-handedly': No punctuation at all between two independent clauses is a fused sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'single-handedly;': A semicolon correctly joins two closely related independent clauses without a coordinating conjunction. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
